# [NSF NCAR](http://ncar.ucar.edu)

![NSF NCAR](/profiles/custom/ncar_ucar_umbrella/themes/custom/koru/libraries/koru-base/img/logo-ncar.png)
![NSF Logo](/profiles/custom/ncar_ucar_umbrella/themes/custom/koru/libraries/koru-base/img/NSF_Official_logo.png)

# [NSF NCAR](https://ncar.ucar.edu)

![NSF NCAR](/profiles/custom/ncar_ucar_umbrella/themes/custom/koru/libraries/koru-base/img/logo-ncar.png)

NSF NCAR is a center of research excellence in Earth system science sponsored by the National Science Foundation.

We provide state-of-the art research, observing and computing facilities, and expert services to get the most out of them.

Improving predictions, from moment to moment, day to day, and decade to decade, is the heart of our work.

Whether you're an undergraduate student or a faculty member, come expand your horizons at NSF NCAR.

NSF NCAR is a center of research excellence in Earth system science sponsored by the National Science Foundation.

We provide state-of-the art research, observing and computing facilities, and expert services to get the most out of them.

Improving predictions, from moment to moment, day to day, and decade to decade, is the heart of our work.

Whether you're an undergraduate student or a faculty member, come expand your horizons at NSF NCAR.

# Observational datasets

## Measurements for tracking change

We provide the research community with access to a range of critically important observational datasets as well as the expertise necessary to make the most of the numbers.

These datasets range from observations of a single thunderstorm collected during a field campaign to global temperature and precipitation measurements stretching back decades. They provide scientists with a range of insights, from the microphysics of cloud formation to the impacts of a changing climate.

![8-hour forecast of pressure and winds](/sites/default/files/big-picture-box/2018-08/NCAR%20data%20BPB.png)

### Access our data services

Explore our vast storehouse of data, including climate datasets, observations of the Sun, and our field campaign archives.

### Climate data expertise

Observational datasets covering a long period of time are a cornerstone of climate research. However, changes in sampling protocols, instrument degradation, and other factors can complicate the detection of climate change signals in the data. NCAR scientists perform analyses to address these issues and provide expertise on dataset use. For example, NCAR scientists use the conservation of energy, mass, and moisture principles to validate observations from many sources.

In addition, NCAR scientists created the [Climate Data Guide](https://climatedataguide.ucar.edu/), a widely used website that communicates the strengths, limitations, and applications of datasets used in climate research. A common application of these datasets is the evaluation of Earth system models, using community tools such as the [Climate Variability Diagnostics Package](http://www.cesm.ucar.edu/working_groups/CVC/cvdp/) developed at NCAR.

[Learn more](http://www.cgd.ucar.edu/cas/)

![Image 1: Climate Data Online promo image](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/images/promos/cdo_cover_hex.png)
Climate Data Online
===================

Climate Data Online (CDO) provides free access to NCDC's archive of global historical weather and climate data in addition to station history information. These data include quality controlled daily, monthly, seasonal, and yearly measurements of temperature, precipitation, wind, and degree days as well as radar data and 30-year Climate Normals. Customers can also order most of these data as [certified hard copies](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/certification) for legal use.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Datasets ### Browse Datasets](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/datasets)

Get orders certified for legal use (requires payment)

*   [Order Status ### Check Status](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/orders)
Check the status of an order that has been placed

*   [Find Help ### Find Help](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/faq)
Find answers to questions about data and ordering

Discover data by

[### Search Tool Search for and access past weather and climate data by station name or identifier, ZIP code, city, county, state, or country.Search Tool»](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/search)[### Mapping Tool Find and view past weather and climate data by station name or identifier, ZIP code, city, county, state, or country.Mapping Tool»](https://gis.ncdc.noaa.gov/maps/ncei/)[### Data Tools Access past weather and climate data using a collection of specialized tools.Data Tools»](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/datatools)

Additional Data Access
----------------------

*   [Climate.gov](https://www.climate.gov/)
The NOAA Climate.gov web portal provides science and services for a climate-smart nation

*   [Drought.gov](https://www.drought.gov/)
The NOAA Drought.gov web portal provides an integrated drought monitoring and forecasting system at federal, state, and local levels

*   [Climate Models](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/weather-climate-models)
NCEI, the National Centers for Environmental Prediction (NCEP), and the Geophysical Fluid Dynamics Laboratory provide remote access to high-volume numerical weather prediction and global climate models and data.

*   [Satellite](https://www.avl.class.noaa.gov/release/index.htm#3)
The NOAA Comprehensive Large Array-data Stewardship System (CLASS) is an electronic library of NOAA environmental data

*   [Climate Data Records](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/climate-data-records)
The NOAA Climate Data Record Program provides a robust, sustainable, and scientifically defensible approach to producing and preserving climate records from satellite data

*   [Publications](https://www.ncdc.noaa.gov/IPS/)
The Image & Publication System (IPS) provides access to Monthly Publications for a variety of datasets along with Serial Publications and Other Documents

*   [Storm Events](https://www.ncdc.noaa.gov/stormevents/)
Search the NCDC Storm Events Database to find various types of storms recorded in your county

*   [Severe Weather](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/severe-weather-data-inventory)
The Severe Weather Data Inventory (SWDI) is an integrated database of severe weather records for the United States

*   [Service Records Retention System (SRRS)](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/weather-climate-models/service-records-retention)
The Service Records Retention System (SRRS) stores weather observations, summaries, forecasts, warnings, and advisories provided by the U.S. National Weather Service (NWS) for public use. Congress mandates that NCEI store and retain SRRS products for a five-year period. SRRS records can be used for accident investigations and litigations.

Getting Started
---------------

*   [Ordering Data](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/faq "Link to FAQ/Help section")
Read the help section for assistance with the Climate Data Online ordering process

*   [Using Web Services](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/webservices/getstarted "Link to guide for getting started with web services")
View the "Getting Started" guide for assistance with Climate Data Online web services

*   [Checking Order Status](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/orders "How do I check my order?")
Find out the status of any order that has been submitted through Climate Data Online

*   [Viewing a List of Stations](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/homr/reports/mshr "Link for documentation for MSHR")
Download the EMSHR Lite edition for a historical view of all station information

DESCRIPTION: The illustration features various tools and technologies utilized for climate research and satellite imagery processing, including polar-orbiting and geostationary satellites, multiple types of aircraft, ground stations, and meteorological equipment.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.climate.gov/sites/default/files/styles/full_width_620_original_image/public/AtmosphericModelSchematic.png?itok=lXBEKHV_
DESCRIPTION: A three-dimensional representation of the Earth features a grid overlay illustrating horizontal and vertical coordinates, alongside a cross-section detailing various physical processes in a climate model, including atmospheric and ocean interactions.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://www.nccs.nasa.gov/sites/default/files/climate_vs_weather.png
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates various sources of observational data, including satellite measurements, cloud observations, sea ice, ocean currents, and atmospheric layers, used for climate modeling.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://climate.nasa.gov/internal_resources/2681/Forecast_evaluation_for_models_run_in_2004_in_F_and_C.jpeg
DESCRIPTION: The graph displays forecast evaluations of temperature changes from 1970 to 2020, comparing model predictions against observed data, with distinct partitions for hindcast and forecast phases.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://storage.googleapis.com/cgiarorg/2023/12/Climate-data-climate-models-and-observational-infrastructure.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A wide-open field with grass and a few small trees under a partly cloudy sky, illustrating weather observation infrastructure and climate data collection efforts.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Observational data includes direct measurements from weather stations, buoys, and ground sensors, providing essential insights into local and global climate patterns over extended periods.

Satellite data encompasses information gathered from orbiting instruments, allowing for comprehensive monitoring of atmospheric conditions, land surface changes, and oceanic processes on a global scale.
Climate reanalysis data combines historical observations with numerical models to produce consistent climate records, enhancing understanding of long-term trends and variability in climate systems.
